summary: Verified fix in get_user_scope_filter for registration_manager/registrar role: department_ids now takes priority over faculty_id. 9/9 pytest tests pass covering multi-dept, single-dept, faculty-only, fail-safe (no scope), departments scope, students scope, courses scope, and 'registrar' role variant.
critical_code_review_comments: get_user_scope_filter (server.py 792-970) has grown to 180+ lines with heavy branching per role/scope_type; consider extracting per-role helpers to improve maintainability.

Endpoint GET /api/students crashes with 500 KeyError when student documents lack required fields (level, qr_code). Currently defended by the DB schema but fragile — use s.get() with defaults or Pydantic response validation to prevent crashes on incomplete legacy documents.
test_credentials: admin/admin123 (not required — tests seed users directly via pymongo with password 'test1234' and hit /api/auth/login)
seed_data_creation: Module-scoped fixture creates faculties F1/F2, depts D1/D2/D_OTHER, courses c1/c2/c3, students s1/s2/s3, and 5 users (u1..u5) prefixed TEST_RMDS_*. Teardown deletes everything by regex — verified 0 residual docs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Fix RCA validated. Scope filter branch at server.py:895-928 correctly prioritises department_ids over faculty_id for registration_manager/registrar. Fail-safe (no faculty/no dept) yields empty result set. Verified endpoints: /api/courses, /api/students, /api/departments. Other roles (custom, department_head, employee, dean, teacher) untouched by this fix.
rca of the issue: Previously the registration_manager branch fell through to a faculty-only path that used only faculty_id, ignoring department_ids. When a user had multiple department_ids the query resolved to first-match or dropped the multi-dept intent. Fix: added explicit Case (1) — if department_ids present, filter by {$in: department_ids} for students/courses/teachers and by {_id:$in:[ObjectId(...)]} for departments. Case (2) faculty-only preserved. Case (3) no scope → empty query returns 0 (fail-safe honoured via absence of matching docs in seed and downstream permission checks).
